text: Porous heterogeneous catalysts constitute a cornerstone in the chemical industry. Here, we present a new Hf-based metal-organic framework (MOF) denoted as Hf-NU-1000, containing Hf6 clusters. This catalyst exhibits high activity for the activation of various different types of epoxides and is used for the quantitative CO2 chemical fixation into five-membered cyclic carbonates under mild conditions, which highlights the potential of this material as an excellent catalyst. Moreover, Hf-NU-1000 is shown to be an efficient multifunctional catalyst for other type of epoxide activation reactions, such as the regioselective and enantioretentive formation of 1,2-bifunctionalized molecules by solvolytic nucleophilic ring opening.
